skill1.kitLine: Affects 2 ally unit(s) with the highest final ATK
Charge Speed ▲ 11.67% of the skill user's Charge Speed for 10 sec
skill1.note: Caster-relative qualifier ('of the skill user's Charge Speed') unreconciled — modeled as flat target-own buff. No caster-relative charge-speed StatKey exists. Documented in caveat; low impact at scope lock.

skill2.kitLine: Affects self. Activates when above 80% HP. Gain continuous Pierce.
skill2.note: Pierce effect IS modeled via hasPierce. The >80% HP gate is UNMODELED but documented in caveat as always-satisfied at scope lock (boss deals no damage to units). Full line also appears in unmodeled.skill2 — the unmodeled entry covers the gate, not the effect. Caveat: 'Re-encode the gate if incoming damage is ever modeled.'

skill2.kitLine: Affects self. Activates when HP falls below 80%. Continuously recover HP by 8.12% of attack damage.
skill2.note: Lifesteal/HP-recovery is inert at scope lock: no incoming damage means HP never drops below 80%, so this branch never fires. Correctly unmodeled.

documentedConfounds.text: skill1: Charge Speed 11.67% modeled as flat target-own buff; kit says '11.67% of the skill user's Charge Speed' (caster-relative) — qualifier unreconciled, no caster-relative StatKey exists.
documentedConfounds.why: At scope lock (skill levels 10/10/10, no cube), Alice's own charge speed stat is fixed, so caster-relative vs flat is a constant offset — low impact. Not the cause of the 0.924 miss.

documentedConfounds.text: skill2: Pierce modeled UNGATED (hasPierce); kit gates it '>80% HP' — always satisfied in v1 (boss deals no damage). Re-encode if incoming damage is ever modeled.
documentedConfounds.why: Gate is always true at scope lock; ungated pierce is behaviorally identical. No gap contribution.

documentedConfounds.text: Override note claims hasPierce 'enables the pierce double-hit (core + body per shot on a core-exposed boss, community-verified)'. Kit-status findings mark this STALE: PIERCE_CORE_DOUBLE=false; pierce = one popup per shot, no double-hit (A23). hasPierce is still correctly retained.
documentedConfounds.why: The note's mechanism description is wrong but the config value (hasPierce: true) is correct. The stale prose does not affect sim output.

documentedConfounds.text: Kit-status findings: '0.91 residual is basis/comp-side (read 1.10-1.12 HOT w/ same model 07-13/14)'. The COLD 0.924 reading is attributed to comp/basis issues, not the model.
documentedConfounds.why: Same model read HOT (1.10–1.12) on a different comp/date. The single COLD data point (n=1, PA MiKa boss Iron) is flagged as comp-side, not a model defect. MAD 0.076 is within the ±8% band.

documentedConfounds.text: Override note: 'materialized 2026-07-16: skill1/skill2/burst auto-filled from the offline parser (blablalink prose) — behavior-identical to the prior runtime parse; NOT hand-verified'.
documentedConfounds.why: Provenance flag only. The parser output matches the prose values exactly (11.67/7/80.15/55.12 all confirmed against char-extract). No behavioral risk.

authorIntentSummary: The override models Alice as a clean self-burst DPS (burst: chargeSpeed 80.15% + ATK 55.12% for 10s) with a party-support skill1 (top-2-ATK allies get chargeSpeed 11.67% + chargeDamage 7% on Full Burst enter). Skill2's pierce is encoded as hasPierce: true (ungated, correct at scope lock); the HP-conditional lifesteal branch is correctly unmodeled as inert. The one known approximation is the caster-relative charge-speed qualifier in skill1, flattened due to no supporting StatKey — documented as low-impact. The COLD 0.924 board reading (n=1) is attributed to comp/basis-side issues, not the model; the same model read HOT on a different comp.
